perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS: The Parties are entering into this NDA in contemplation of exploring mutual business opportunities and /or joint educational or research activities . In the course of meetings, communi - cations, correspondence s and discussions, certain Confidential Information (as hereinafter defined) may be disclosed by USM and/or ABC to each other for the Purpose (as hereinafter defined).
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS: The Parties are entering into this NDA in contemplation of exploring mutual business opportunities and /or joint educational or research activities. In the course of meetings, communications, correspondence s and discussions, certain Proprietary Data (as hereinafter defined) may be disclosed by USM and/or ABC to each other for the Objective (as hereinafter defined).
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Proprietary Data' and 'Purpose' with 'Objective', creating confusion as to whether they are interchangeable and leading to a contradiction. Throughout the document, the definition of Confidential Information will be used, while 'Proprietary Data' is introduced without definition. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding which information is covered by the NDA, and which activities are permitted under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this NDA and the appendices hereto, if any, the following words and expressions shall have the following meaning ± "Confidential Information" means any and all technical and non-technical information including patent, copyright, trade secret, know-how and proprietary information, techniques, sketches, drawings, diagrams, methods, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to a technology or invention, and includes, without limitation, its respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing manufacturing, customer list, business forecasts, sales and merchandising and marketing plans and information designated in writing to be confidential or by its nature intended to be for the sole knowledge of the Receiving Party (as hereinafter defined) or if orally given in the circumstances of confidence or confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary for the Purpose; that is Legal Office Universiti Sains Malaysia ~ PP11 Page 2 conveyed by the Disclosing Party (as hereinafter defined) to the Receiving Party, in written, oral, digital, magnetic, photographic and/or whatsoever forms;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' and 'Purpose' with 'Objective'. The contradicted text specifically defines 'Confidential Information' and refers to the 'Purpose'. Since 'Proprietary Data' and 'Objective' are introduced without definition and are not used consistently throughout the document, this creates a contradiction. The agreement no longer has a consistent definition of what information is covered, and creates uncertainty as to which activities are permitted. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: the Receiving Party shall have first obtained the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: the Recipient shall have first obtained the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Receiving Party' is replaced with 'Recipient', but 'Receiving Party' is defined in the definitions section. Throughout the document, the definition of Receiving Party will be used, while 'Recipient' is introduced without definition. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this NDA and the appendices hereto, if any, the following words and expressions shall have the following meaning ± "Confidential Information" means any and all technical and non-technical information including patent, copyright, trade secret, know-how and proprietary information, techniques, sketches, drawings, diagrams, methods, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to a technology or invention, and includes, without limitation, its respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing manufacturing, customer list, business forecasts, sales and merchandising and marketing plans and information designated in writing to be confidential or by its nature intended to be for the sole knowledge of the Receiving Party (as hereinafter defined) or if orally given in the circumstances of confidence or confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary for the Purpose; that is Legal Office Universiti Sains Malaysia ~ PP11 Page 2 conveyed by the Disclosing Party (as hereinafter defined) to the Receiving Party, in written, oral, digital, magnetic, photographic and/or whatsoever forms;
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term 'Receiving Party', which is explicitly defined in the 'DEFINITIONS' section of the contract. The changed text replaces 'Receiving Party' with 'Recipient'. This creates a contradiction because the 'DEFINITIONS' section now references a term ('Receiving Party') that is no longer consistently used throughout the document, while 'Recipient' is used without definition. The definition of Receiving party is used throughout the document which is contradictory to the term 'Recipient'.

perturbation.original_text: In relation to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party undertakes - (a) to maintain the Confidential Information in strict confidence ;
perturbation.changed_text: In relation to the Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party undertakes - (a) to maintain the Sensitive Data in strict confidence ;
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Sensitive Data' and 'Proprietary Information', creating confusion as to whether they are interchangeable and leading to a contradiction. Throughout the document, the definition of Confidential Information will be used, while 'Sensitive Data' and 'Proprietary Information' are introduced without definition. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding which information is covered by the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this NDA and the appendices hereto, if any, the following words and expressions shall have the following meaning ± "Confidential Information" means any and all technical and non-technical information including patent, copyright, trade secret, know-how and proprietary information, techniques, sketches, drawings, diagrams, methods, processes, apparatus, equipment, algorithms, software programs, software source documents, and formulae related to a technology or invention, and includes, without limitation, its respective information concerning research, experimental work, development, design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing manufacturing, customer list, business forecasts, sales and merchandising and marketing plans and information designated in writing to be confidential or by its nature intended to be for the sole knowledge of the Receiving Party (as hereinafter defined) or if orally given in the circumstances of confidence or confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary for the Purpose; that is Legal Office Universiti Sains Malaysia ~ PP11 Page 2 conveyed by the Disclosing Party (as hereinafter defined) to the Receiving Party, in written, oral, digital, magnetic, photographic and/or whatsoever forms;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' and introduces 'Sensitive Data'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information'. Because the changed text uses new terms without defining them, while the contradicted text relies on a specific definition of 'Confidential Information', the change creates a contradiction. Specifically, it's now unclear whether 'Sensitive Data' and 'Proprietary Information' are also covered by the definition of 'Confidential Information' in section 1. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, leading to conflicting obligations and confusion.

perturbation.original_text: In the event disclosure of the Confidential Information is made by the Receiving Party to any third party, the Receiving Party shall ensure that the third party undertakes to comply strictly to the conditions and the non -disclosure obligations of the Parties under this NDA and for the aforesaid purpose, the Receiving Party shall cause the third party to execute an undertaking in the format set out in Appendix III hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event disclosure of the Confidential Data is made by the Receiving Party to any third party, the Receiving Party shall ensure that the third party undertakes to comply strictly to the conditions and the non -disclosure obligations of the Signatories under this NDA and for the aforesaid purpose, the Receiving Party shall cause the third party to execute an undertaking in the format set out in Appendix III hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Confidential Data' and 'Parties' replaced with 'Signatories', creating confusion as to whether they are interchangeable and leading to a contradiction. Throughout the document, the definition of Confidential Information will be used, while 'Confidential Data' and 'Signatories' are introduced without definition. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. perturbation.original_text: Return of Confidential Information The Rece iving Party shall immediately on request by the Disclosing Party return all or any part of the Confidential Information as shall be determined by the Disclosing Party and shall (at the Disclosing Party's option) -
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Proprietary Information The Recipient shall immediately on request by the Disclosing Party return all or any part of the Proprietary Information as shall be determined by the Disclosing Party and shall (at the Disclosing Party's option) -

This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that no mon etary damages shall be sufficient to avoid or compensate the unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential I nformation and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threatened use or disclosure of such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that no mon etary damages shall be sufficient to avoid or compensate the unauthorized use or disclosure of the Proprietary Data and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threatened use or disclosure of such Proprietary Data.

This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty.
